steps.title: Set up equations from percentages and eliminate c
steps.content:
Translate to equations: $a = 23.52(b + c)$ and $b = 0.84c$.
From the second equation, $c = \frac{b}{0.84}$. Substitute into the first: $$a = 23.52\left(b + \frac{b}{0.84}\right) = 23.52b\left(1 + \frac{1}{0.84}\right)$$

steps.title: Simplify the coefficient to find a in terms of b
steps.content: Combine the terms in parentheses: $$1 + \frac{1}{0.84} = \frac{1.84}{0.84} = \frac{46}{21}$$Compute the product: $$a = 23.52 \times \frac{46}{21} \times b = 51.52b$$

steps.title: Convert to percentage form
steps.content: Since $a = 51.52b$, we have $a$ is $51.52 \times 100\% = 5,152\%$ of $b$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
